item.text: The LSP checklist is targeting substitutability at public seams, not behavior changes. I’m comparing the already-edited `bets` package to reuse the same pattern in `markets` rather than inventing a different interpretation.

I’ve confirmed the pattern from the earlier `bets` LSP work: remove unnecessary factory/assembler indirection, make option setters preserve the default contract even when given `nil`, and make zero-value test doubles fail predictably instead of panicking or silently changing semantics. I’m applying that same pattern to `markets` now.

I’ve updated the domain-facing definitions and option handling. The remaining work is in the test doubles: they need explicit fallback behavior so zero-value substitutes keep the same contract instead of depending on hidden setup.

The core test-double files are patched. I’m adding the last LSP-specific coverage in the remaining listed tests, then I’ll run targeted Go tests and clean up any fallout from the stricter zero-value behavior.

item.text: `go test` is blocked by this environment’s default module cache path, not by the code. I’m rerunning with a local cache inside the workspace so I can get actual compile/test feedback without changing repository state.

item.text:
task_id: `PR581-020`  
status: `completed`

Aligned the scoped `markets` LSP seams by simplifying top-level domain definitions, preserving default service behavior under `nil` option substitution, and hardening scoped test doubles so zero-value substitutes fail predictably instead of changing contracts. The main code changes are in [errors.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/errors.go), [models.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/models.go), and [service.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/service.go). The scoped tests were updated in [service_details_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/service_details_test.go), [service_listbystatus_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/service_listbystatus_test.go), [service_marketbets_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/service_marketbets_test.go), [service_probability_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/service_probability_test.go), [service_resolve_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/service_resolve_test.go), and [service_search_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/markets/service_search_test.go).

Assumption: I applied the same LSP interpretation already used in the adjacent `bets` work, namely that substitutable collaborators must preserve the default contract when `nil` or zero-valued rather than introducing special-case behavior.
